**Kai Madison Trump** (born May 12, 2007) is an American [social media](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Social_media "Social media") personality. A member of the [Trump family](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trump_family "Trump family"), she is the eldest child of [Donald Trump](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donald_Trump_Jr. "Donald Trump Jr.") and [Vanessa Haydon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vanessa_Trump "Vanessa Trump") and the eldest grandchild of U.S.[Donald Trump](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump"). She gave a speech in support of her grandfather's [2024](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donald_Trump_2024_presidential_campaign "Donald Trump 2024 presidential campaign") at the [2024 Republican National Convention](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2024_Republican_National_Convention "2024 Republican National Convention"),[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-1) subsequently becoming a social media personality. 
## Early life
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Kai_Trump&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]
Further information: [Trump family](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trump_family "Trump family")
Kai Madison Trump was born on May[New York City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City "New York City").[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-2) She is the first child of [Donald Trump](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donald_Trump_Jr. "Donald Trump Jr.") and [Vanessa Haydon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vanessa_Trump "Vanessa Trump"), and the first grandchild of [Donald Trump](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump").[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-people-3) She is named after her great-grandfather, Danish jazz musician [Kai Ewans](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Ewans "Kai Ewans").[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-4)[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-5) She is the oldest of five siblings.[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-people-3) She has signed up for the [University of Miami](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Miami "University of Miami") in [Coral Gables, Florida](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coral_Gables,_Florida "Coral Gables, Florida"), and may become a member of the class of 2030.[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-6)
## Golf
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Kai_Trump&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Golf")]
Trump is a competitive golfer[[7]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-7) and has played for [The Benjamin School](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Benjamin_School "The Benjamin School") in [Palm Beach Gardens](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palm_Beach_Gardens,_Florida "Palm Beach Gardens, Florida").[[8]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-SI.com-8) She won the 2022[Trump International Palm Beach](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trump_International_Palm_Beach "Trump International Palm Beach"), where she recorded a competitive [handicap](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Handicap_\(golf\) "Handicap \(golf\)") of +0.5.[[9]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-:2-9)
In August 2024, she verbally committed to attend the [University of Miami](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Miami_Hurricanes_women%27s_golf "Miami Hurricanes women's golf") to play collegiate golf as a member of the 2026 signing class.[[10]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-10)
As of February 2025, Trump reportedly had a [name, image, and likeness valuation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Student_athlete_compensation "Student athlete compensation") of $1.2[Callaway Golf Company](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Callaway_Golf_Company "Callaway Golf Company")[[8]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-SI.com-8) and [TaylorMade Golf](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TaylorMade_Golf "TaylorMade Golf").[[11]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-11)
In March 2025, Trump competed in the [Junior Invitational](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Junior_Invitational "Junior Invitational"). She finished 24th[[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-12)[[13]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-13)
Trump accepted an invitation to make her debut on the [LPGA Tour](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LPGA_Tour "LPGA Tour") in November 2025, at [The Annika](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Annika "The Annika") sponsored by [Annika Sörenstam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Annika_S%C3%B6renstam "Annika Sörenstam").[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-14) She shot rounds of 83 and 75 to finish last in a field of 108, and miss the [cut](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cut_\(golf\) "Cut \(golf\)").[[15]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-15)[[16]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-16)
## Politics
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Kai_Trump&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Politics")]
[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:President_Trump_Gaggles_with_the_Press_Before_Departing_the_White_House,_Sep._26,_2025.webm "Play video")[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:President_Trump_Gaggles_with_the_Press_Before_Departing_the_White_House,_Sep._26,_2025.webm)Trump with her grandfather while he speaks with White House press in September 2025
Trump gave a speech at the [2024 Republican National Convention](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2024_Republican_National_Convention "2024 Republican National Convention") in [Milwaukee](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Milwaukee "Milwaukee"), focusing on her relationship with her grandfather. In the speech, she characterized him as "just a normal grandpa" and called him an inspiration.[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-17)[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-18) [John Mulholland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Mulholland_\(journalist\) "John Mulholland \(journalist\)"), the former _[Guardian US](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guardian_US "Guardian US")_ editor, called the speech "part of a politically calculated makeover" while political commentator and regular [CNN](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CNN "CNN") contributor [Van Jones](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Van_Jones "Van Jones") said that "The granddaughter came out and opened up our hearts" and said that she did a good job of humanizing Donald Trump and making him appear "likeable".[[19]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-Kosowan-19)[[20]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-20)[[21]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-21)
## Social media
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Kai_Trump&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Social media")]
Following her appearance at the RNC in July 2024, Trump's [Instagram](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Instagram "Instagram") follower count rose from 125,000 to 185,000[[19]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-Kosowan-19)
Trump received significant attention for [vlogging](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vlog "Vlog") the celebration of Donald Trump's victory in the [2024 United States presidential election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2024_United_States_presidential_election "2024 United States presidential election")[[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-22)[[23]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-23) and for saying that [Elon Musk](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elon_Musk "Elon Musk") had "attained uncle status" on November[extensive support of Donald Trump's campaign](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Political_activities_of_Elon_Musk "Political activities of Elon Musk").[[24]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-24)[[25]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-25)
As of October[YouTube](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/YouTube "YouTube") channel has 1.30[TikTok](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TikTok "TikTok") has 3.7[X](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/X_\(social_network\) "X \(social network\)") has 906,800[[26]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-26)[[27]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-27)[[28]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-28) Following the election, she was highlighted as a potentially important influencer in improving Donald Trump's popularity among the young, and a powerful ally on social media.[[29]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-29)[[30]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-30)
During the [2026 Iran war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_war "2026 Iran war"), she posted a YouTube video entitled "I Brought My Secret Service to [Erewhon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Erewhon_\(store\) "Erewhon \(store\)")", one of the most expensive grocery store chains in the United States.[[31]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-high-end-31) The video was criticized by _[The Guardian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")_ columnist [Marina Hyde](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marina_Hyde "Marina Hyde"),[[32]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-32) [The Lincoln Project](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Lincoln_Project "The Lincoln Project"),[[33]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-33)[[34]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-34) and general social media commenters as tone-deaf.[[35]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-35)[[31]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kai_Trump#cite_note-high-end-31)
